title: sedum ternatum
intro: Sedum ternatum, also known as Wood's sedum or woodland stonecrop, is a species of succulent plant that belongs to the family Crassulaceae. It is native to eastern North America and is known for its small, white flowers and rounded, fleshy leaves. Sedum ternatum has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and has been used to treat various health issues such as wounds, fever, and respiratory problems. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and support overall health and well-being.
